keyRules: Registration is required for items weighing 250 g or more, or less than 250 g if they have a camera
EU registration from your home country is valid—no new Norwegian registration is required
Liability insurance is normally required for weights of 250 g or more (no requirement for weights under 250 g in the open category)
A permit is required within 5 km of an airport—use the Ninox Drone app
Downtown Oslo (R102) is a permanent restricted area—requires permission from the Civil Aviation Authority
Remote Identification (Remote ID) is required for C1–C3-class drones
Always check protected areas and national parks separately
importantNotes: Foreign pilots with cameras or sensors: Notify the NSM (National Security Authority) before flying
Svalbard has its own very strict rules—see the Governor’s website
Norway fully complies with the EASA Open Category (Open Category A1/A2/A3)

fields.disclaimerText: Always check official sources before flying. This app is a resource—not a legal source. Rules are subject to change without notice.
